message:
🐛 Fix initialization race condition in cache worker (#2306)

* 🐛 Fix initialization race condition in cache worker

Messages arriving before initDatabase() completes got null from
handleGet because db was still null. Queue incoming messages during
initialization and drain the queue once ready.

Fixes #2230

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Queue payload not MessageEvent, rename dbReady to initComplete

Address Copilot review:
- Store just WorkerRequest payload in pendingMessages queue instead
  of the full MessageEvent wrapper.
- Rename dbReady to initComplete since setting it to true on failure
  was misleading — the flag tracks init completion, not DB health.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix worker init error reporting and bound pending queue

Address Copilot review comments on PR #2306:

1. Bound the pending message queue to MAX_PENDING_MESSAGES (1000) to
   prevent unbounded memory growth if SQLite init stalls. Messages
   arriving after the limit get an error response instead of being
   silently queued.

2. On initDatabase() failure, post {type:'init-error'} instead of
   {type:'ready'} so the RPC layer rejects the ready promise and
   the consumer (initCacheWorker) correctly falls back to IndexedDB.
   Queued messages are rejected with error responses rather than
   processed against a null database.

3. The pending queue already stores WorkerRequest payloads (not full
   MessageEvent objects), which was addressed in the original PR.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
